title: Cnicus tchefouensis
intro: Cnicus tchefouensis, also known as Tche fou en sèche or Tche fou, is a type of spiny, thistle-like plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. It is traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cnicus tchefouensis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Cnicus tchefouensis is often prepared as a tea or dried powder, and it is also used in herbal soups and infusions to promote health and well-being.
taxonomy: Cnicus tchefouensis is classified under the family Asteraceae and is a type of thistle native to China. This plant is also known as Tche Fou thistle. Cnicus tchefouensis is a species within the Cnicus genus, which consists of several species of thistles. The classification of Cnicus tchefouensis is further categorized as Cnicus tchefouensis (Franch.) Hand.-Mazz. under the binomial nomenclature system. It is a plant of the Rosidae clade, one of the major clades of flowering plants.
